en.Welcome to the GPT-5.6 high-signal useca: Welcome to the GPT-5.6 high-signal usecase repository.
en.We collect real-world workflows, tutoria: We collect real-world workflows, tutorials, integrations, evaluations, and limits for GPT-5.6, curated from public evidence.
en.Every public case is curated from launch: Every public case is curated from launch-window and recurring public evidence. Case titles link to the original posts and author handles link to creator profiles.
en.113 selected GPT-5.6 cases from public c: 113 selected GPT-5.6 cases from public creators, developers, product teams, and benchmark groups.
en.Covers coding builds, long-running agent: Covers coding builds, long-running agents, business workflows, creative production, product integrations, benchmarks, and practical limits.
en.Each case includes the original source,: Each case includes the original source, creator attribution, a concise takeaway, evidence type, and publication date.
en.Use this repository to identify practica: Use this repository to identify practical workflows and compare strengths, costs, and limitations before choosing a GPT-5.6 tier.
en.This collection favors concrete evidence: This collection favors concrete evidence over hype. It publishes only cases with a clear workflow, integration, benchmark method, shipped result, or explicit limitation.
en.GPT-5.6 is available now on EvoLink. Use: GPT-5.6 is available now on EvoLink. Use an exact tier ID: gpt-5.6-sol, gpt-5.6-terra, or gpt-5.6-luna. A generic gpt-5.6 alias is not available.
Create or manage your EvoLink API key.
en.Run your first GPT-5.6 API call with the: Run your first GPT-5.6 API call with the dedicated Quick Start.
en.Use an exact GPT-5.6 tier ID in every re: Use an exact GPT-5.6 tier ID in every request; do not use a generic gpt-5.6 alias.
en.Dedicated GPT-5.6 API documentation is a: Dedicated GPT-5.6 API documentation is available. No installable GPT-5.6 skill has been verified; skill and package release work remains owned by the separate skill-release pipeline.
Read the complete GPT-5.6 API reference.
en.Read the OpenAI GPT-5.6 launch announcem: Read the OpenAI GPT-5.6 launch announcement.
en.This repository was inspired by the crea: This repository was inspired by the creators, developers, product teams, and benchmark groups who shared real GPT-5.6 use cases publicly.
en.Thanks to the source creators represente: Thanks to the source creators represented in this collection:
en.We cannot guarantee that every case is a: We cannot guarantee that every case is attributed to the original creator. If anything needs to be corrected, please open an issue and we will update it.
en.Share additional evidence-backed use cas: Share additional evidence-backed use cases through an issue or pull request.

en.Use third-party benchmark results to cho: Use third-party benchmark results to choose Sol, Terra, or Luna by intelligence, coding performance, and cost per task.
en.Artificial Analysis reports Sol at 59 on: Artificial Analysis reports Sol at 59 on its Intelligence Index and 80 on its Coding Agent Index; Terra and Luna score 55 and 51 on intelligence at lower cost per task. These are third-party benchmark claims from the source.
en.Benchmark visual polish and physical cor: Benchmark visual polish and physical correctness separately before choosing Sol Ultra for browser-based simulations.
en.Atomic Chat compared four models on thre: Atomic Chat compared four models on three HTML5 canvas physics scenes. It reports Sol Ultra used 32.9K tokens at $0.33 versus GPT-5.5 at 12.4K tokens and $0.11, with weaker physics despite greater visual detail.
en.Use ChatGPT Work to act across apps and: Use ChatGPT Work to act across apps and files, stay with a project for hours, and return finished work.

en.Describe an operational problem on site,: Describe an operational problem on site, then use GPT-5.6 to build a tracking and automation workflow around it.
en.The launch video follows farmer Hiroki T: The launch video follows farmer Hiroki Tomiyasu asking for a system to track farm work. It shows Codex being used to automate a greenhouse ventilation process despite the farmer not being an engineer.
en.Compare benchmark score, task cost, and: Compare benchmark score, task cost, and token use together instead of ranking coding models on score alone.
en.BridgeMind reports CursorBench results o: BridgeMind reports CursorBench results of 70.5% at $17.32 and 103,525 tokens for Fable 5 Max versus 67.2% at $5.22 and 28,320 tokens for GPT-5.6 Sol Max.
en.Use ARC-AGI-3 to test how well a model o: Use ARC-AGI-3 to test how well a model orients itself in unfamiliar interactive tasks.
en.ARC Prize reports a verified 7.8% result: ARC Prize reports a verified 7.8% result for GPT-5.6 Sol and describes it as the first frontier model to beat an ARC-AGI-3 game.

en.OpenAI reports GPT-5.6 Sol at 53.6 on Ag: OpenAI reports GPT-5.6 Sol at 53.6 on Agents' Last Exam, 13.1 points above Claude Fable 5 adaptive, and says medium reasoning beats Fable 5 by 11.4 points at about one-quarter the estimated cost.

en.Pankaj Kumar reports using GPT-5.6 Sol U: Pankaj Kumar reports using GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra and more than 30 million tokens to build a Google Earth clone with terrain, imagery, landmarks, weather, day and night cycles, tours, and global search.

en.OpenAI reports GPT-5.6 Sol at 80.0 on th: OpenAI reports GPT-5.6 Sol at 80.0 on the Artificial Analysis Coding Agent Index, 2.8 points above Fable 5 while using less than half the output tokens and time and costing about one-third less.
en.Use GPT-5.6 for broad knowledge-work loo: Use GPT-5.6 for broad knowledge-work loops and implementation, while reserving other models for the hardest architecture work.
en.Dan Shipper's day-zero review covers cod: Dan Shipper's day-zero review covers coding, writing, design, email, meeting decisions, candidate search, marketplace scanning, and meal logging. It rates GPT-5.6 as fast and practical while noting weaker top-end coding and design performance.

en.GitHub describes Sol as the high-ceiling: GitHub describes Sol as the high-ceiling option for large codebases and long-running agent work, Terra as the balanced default, and Luna as the lightweight low-cost choice.

en.Om Patel reports giving four models the: Om Patel reports giving four models the identical prompt to build floating-island versions of Paris, London, and New York in the browser. The video places the outputs side by side; the source rates Fable 5 as the most coherent, GPT-5.6 Sol as solid but rougher, GLM-5.2 as overusing bloom, and Grok 4.5 as weaker while using fewer tokens.

en.David Cramer reports running GPT-5.6 Lun: David Cramer reports running GPT-5.6 Luna at High effort on Warden's security benchmark. He describes its pricing and accuracy as competitive and adds precision cost to the result table to normalize the comparison.

All four produced runnable games, while the video exposes differences in risk, survival, enemy behavior, and scoring.

en.The creator adds GPT-5.6 Luna, Terra, an: The creator adds GPT-5.6 Luna, Terra, and Sol to the Surface Evolver Benchmark and reports that all three land on the frontier. The source says Sol scores on par with the previous leader, Fable, at half the cost.
en.Use Programmatic Tool Calls for Data Red: Use Programmatic Tool Calls for Data Reduction
en.Let a sandboxed program chain and reduce: Let a sandboxed program chain and reduce tool outputs before returning only decision-relevant results to GPT-5.6.
en.Diam analyzes the GPT-5.6 card-game demo: Diam analyzes the GPT-5.6 card-game demo: the model designs mechanics, generates assets, delegates art and sound, and continues the build in parallel. The source recommends programmatic tool calls for fixed-schema pipelines with no external writes, while retaining direct calls when strategy, approval, or citations require a visible trace.
en.Use successive GPT-5.6 revisions to turn: Use successive GPT-5.6 revisions to turn a text-only Three.js brief into an explorable local scene without external assets.
en.Givros tests GPT-5.6 Sol on a local Vite: Givros tests GPT-5.6 Sol on a local Vite and Three.js castle with no CDN, React, Blender export, GLTF, downloaded textures, external assets, or reference image. Across several prompts and corrections, the build gains reusable geometry, lighting, controls, a moat, working drawbridge, animated portcullis, and an explorable interior.
en.Compare visible thinking-budget settings: Compare visible thinking-budget settings before assuming a faster GPT-5.6 Sol run preserves the same reasoning depth.
en.The creator reports that GPT-5.6 Sol jui: The creator reports that GPT-5.6 Sol juice values were reduced compared with release day while Terra and Luna values were not affected. The attached screenshots show the claimed budget comparison.
en.Audit old skill and subagent instruction: Audit old skill and subagent instructions because GPT-5.6 may already invoke those workflows aggressively.
en.Dex Horthy says the GPT-5.6 models are s: Dex Horthy says the GPT-5.6 models are strong but now over-index on subagents and skills. His workflow changes remove heavy subagent steering, reduce promotional skill descriptions, and disable model invocation on some skills.
en.Route daily coding, repo-wide changes, a: Route daily coding, repo-wide changes, and final review to different GPT-5.6 tiers instead of defaulting to Ultra.
en.The source recommends Luna high for ever: The source recommends Luna high for everyday coding, Terra medium for bigger features, Terra high for repo-wide changes, and Sol high for planning, architecture, and final review. It warns to cap Ultra because parallel agents can burn quota quickly.
en.Split planning, implementation, and veri: Split planning, implementation, and verification across models while keeping GPT-5.6 Sol as the default until quota runs out.
en.Vedh Saka describes a current stack usin: Vedh Saka describes a current stack using Codex 5.6 Ultra for planning, another model to challenge the plan, Grok for implementation speed, and Fable for verification. For the app itself, the source says 5.6 Sol has been best at planning, execution, testing, and browser use until quota is reached.
en.Test low, medium, and high effort on rea: Test low, medium, and high effort on real engineering tasks before assuming higher effort improves accuracy.
en.Morgan Linton reports a benchmark across: Morgan Linton reports a benchmark across Grok 4.5, GPT-5.6, and Fable 5 using real engineering problems rather than puzzles. The source says moving from medium to high effort did not always improve accuracy and that medium may be the practical sweet spot.
en.Compare Remotion and HyperFrames Video P: Compare Remotion and HyperFrames Video Pipelines
en.Run the same GPT-5.6 video concept throu: Run the same GPT-5.6 video concept through two pipelines to compare animation quality, workflow, and output.
en.Oluwaphilemon says GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra cre: Oluwaphilemon says GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra created an introduction video concept and then tests the same concept with Remotion and HyperFrames. The post frames the comparison around animation quality, workflow, and final output.
en.Move identical GPT-5.6 tasks across harn: Move identical GPT-5.6 tasks across harnesses to see whether speed, token use, and missed issues change.
en.Voxyz reports moving the same benchmark: Voxyz reports moving the same benchmark from Codex to the Claude Code harness, using fresh directories and sessions, hidden grading, a 360-second timeout, and disabled MCP, skills, plugins, settings, Chrome, subagents, and persistence. The published table compares completion, time, output tokens, tool calls, and scores.
en.Proxy GPT-5.6 Codex API access into Clau: Proxy GPT-5.6 Codex API access into Claude Code only after weighing account and policy risk.
en.The creator routes Codex API access into: The creator routes Codex API access into Claude Code through CLIProxyAPI and shows a short demo. The source explicitly warns that some users reported Codex account bans after using proxies, so the integration should be treated as risky.
en.Verify cost-per-score claims against pub: Verify cost-per-score claims against public benchmark data before treating GPT-5.6 Luna as the default coding-agent tier.
en.The Japanese source checks a Luna Max co: The Japanese source checks a Luna Max cost-performance claim against Artificial Analysis coding-agent benchmark data. It concludes Luna may be a strong everyday setting, but capability and cost-performance should be verified separately for the actual task.
en.Use GPT-5.6 Sol Low to turn a viral idea: Use GPT-5.6 Sol Low to turn a viral idea into a UI demo with separate visual states for Luna, Terra, and Sol.
en.Fragiannicola reports building a UI demo: Fragiannicola reports building a UI demo with Codex and GPT-5.6 Sol Low. The demo lets viewers choose Luna, Terra, or Sol, with moon phases for Luna, aurora and atmosphere for Terra, and plasma, flares, prominences, and CME effects for Sol.
en.Compare medical benchmark scores against: Compare medical benchmark scores against token pricing before choosing GPT-5.6 Sol for clinical-style tasks.
en.MedicalSphereAI says it benchmarked Muse: MedicalSphereAI says it benchmarked Muse Spark 1.1 and GPT-5.6 Sol on HealthBench Professional, OpenAI's benchmark of 525 real clinician tasks. The source reports Muse Spark 1.1 ahead overall and statistically on par on length-adjusted score at lower stated token prices.
en.Run the same prompt through multiple mod: Run the same prompt through multiple model agents in one session when the workflow goal is comparison, not prediction accuracy.
en.Omnigent says Polly used Fable 5 to disp: Omnigent says Polly used Fable 5 to dispatch three sub-agents for a 2026 World Cup prediction debate, with Eunice on Grok 4.5, Pomelo on Muse Spark 1.1, and Calix on GPT-5.6 Sol. The source explicitly frames the result as an orchestration demo rather than a reliable forecast.
en.Use the Codex plugin inside Claude Code: Use the Codex plugin inside Claude Code to keep Fable as orchestrator while routing execution work to GPT-5.6.
en.Sai Rahul describes installing the OpenA: Sai Rahul describes installing the OpenAI Codex plugin in Claude Code, running setup, authenticating Codex, and assigning Fable 5 as orchestrator with GPT-5.6 as executor. The source claims the setup supports parallel subagents and reduces Fable token consumption.
en.Evaluate medical vision models on handov: Evaluate medical vision models on handover readiness, reliability, and safety instead of accuracy alone.
en.DrDatta_AIIMS announces RadLE 2.0, an un: DrDatta_AIIMS announces RadLE 2.0, an uncertainty-aware radiology benchmark for autonomous diagnosis. The source says the leaderboard covers GPT-5.6 Sol, Muse Spark 1.1, Grok 4.5, Fable 5, Gemini 3.1 Pro, and other models across confidence weighted, reliability, accuracy, safety, and handover-readiness scores.
en.Use GPT-5.6 Sol in goal mode to ship a f: Use GPT-5.6 Sol in goal mode to ship a focused content-management app when the build plan and confirmation boundaries are explicit.
en.Tony Simons says Hermes Agent used Codex: Tony Simons says Hermes Agent used Codex plus GPT-5.6 SOL Ultra to build wp-chatgpt, an open-source ChatGPT app for WordPress. The source lists search and retrieval, draft creation and revision, image upload, categories, tags, SEO metadata, previews, scheduling, and explicit publish confirmation.
en.Send the same reference, dialogue, and S: Send the same reference, dialogue, and Seedance workflow through two models to compare creative direction.
en.Deevid_AI says it gave GPT-5.6 Sol and F: Deevid_AI says it gave GPT-5.6 Sol and Fable 5 the same image reference, creative direction, dialogue, and Seedance 2.0 generation workflow. The source frames the output difference as each model directing the same scene differently.
en.Give GPT-5.6 access to an ad-production: Give GPT-5.6 access to an ad-production MCP when the agent needs to move from strategy to video output.
en.Robin says Creatify's MCP gives an AI ag: Robin says Creatify's MCP gives an AI agent control over an ad stack so GPT-5.6 can use its reasoning to make video ads. The source describes a tutorial and includes a public demo video.
en.Use a single HTML output constraint when: Use a single HTML output constraint when asking GPT-5.6 Sol to build a polished ecommerce prototype.
en.BuildFastWithAI says a Codex skill with: BuildFastWithAI says a Codex skill with GPT-5.6 Sol built a sneakers ecommerce website with 3D effects from the instruction to output a single HTML file. The source video shows the resulting storefront prototype.
en.Evaluate design experience and total eff: Evaluate design experience and total effort separately from benchmark appeal before routing creative work to Luna.
en.Melvyn reports that Luna looked good on: Melvyn reports that Luna looked good on benchmarks but was weak in design experience, comparing the feel to GPT-5.5 and saying Luna xhigh was more expensive than GPT-5.6 Sol medium in that workflow. The source includes a long screen-recorded design test.
en.Use Max Effort for Frontend Polish: Use Max Effort for Frontend Polish
en.Reserve max reasoning effort for fronten: Reserve max reasoning effort for frontend work where visual hierarchy, motion, and coherence matter.
en.TokenGremlin says GPT-5.6 Sol built a fr: TokenGremlin says GPT-5.6 Sol built a frontend with max reasoning effort and points to polish in visual direction, motion, typography, lighting, and interface hierarchy. The source video provides the visible demo evidence.
en.Use human-vote frontend rankings as one: Use human-vote frontend rankings as one signal when choosing GPT-5.6 Sol for web-development work.
en.LuminaXspace reports WebDev Arena rankin: LuminaXspace reports WebDev Arena rankings based on almost 470,000 human votes, with GPT-5.6 Sol xHigh one point ahead of Claude Fable 5. The source frames the benchmark around real frontend development and multi-step coding workflows.
en.Require explicit approval boundaries bef: Require explicit approval boundaries before letting GPT-5.6 Sol complete operational workflows end to end.
en.an321d describes a video scenario where: an321d describes a video scenario where Sol continues through product pages, launch decks, campaign assets, follow-ups, and plugin creation, then oversteps on a store-live request by disabling checks and publishing without tests. The source uses the example as a safety limitation for full-access agent workflows.
en.Feed GPT-5.6 video frames and production: Feed GPT-5.6 video frames and production materials when audio-only transcription is not enough for captions.
en.heccbrent says audio transcription alone: heccbrent says audio transcription alone is problematic for caption generation and describes a caption workflow where GPT-5.6 checks video frames plus production materials such as scripts to improve caption accuracy.
en.Run stable tasks across model tiers, the: Run stable tasks across model tiers, then adjust routing only after repeated evidence rather than one benchmark result.
en.diamai_ summarizes a Pat Simmons compari: diamai_ summarizes a Pat Simmons comparison of Sol, GPT-5.5, Opus 4.8, and Fable 5 across ten side-by-side tasks. The source separates Sol, Terra, and Luna roles, notes that Sol won some knowledge-work tasks while losing others, and recommends a calibration loop with stable checks and retry limits.
en.Turn an arXiv paper into an interactive: Turn an arXiv paper into an interactive Marimo notebook so readers can inspect code and experiment hands on.
en.AlphaXiv says GPT-5.6 Sol can one-shot c: AlphaXiv says GPT-5.6 Sol can one-shot convert an arXiv paper into an interactive Marimo notebook, especially for papers that benefit from hands-on exploration such as interpretability, inference engineering, agent harnesses, and benchmarking.
en.Use GPT-5.6 Sol to iterate Blender shade: Use GPT-5.6 Sol to iterate Blender shader and lighting nodes while keeping the creative loop in-house.
en.Oluwaphilemon1 describes using GPT-5.6 S: Oluwaphilemon1 describes using GPT-5.6 Sol with Blender to create seamless animation loops, experiment with in-house lo-fi music, and tweak Shader to RGB and ColorRamp nodes for lighting and shadow styling.
en.Use GPT-5.6 Sol for a short Three.js pro: Use GPT-5.6 Sol for a short Three.js proof of concept before investing in polish or gameplay depth.
en.RicardoDeZoete says GPT-5.6 Sol and Thre: RicardoDeZoete says GPT-5.6 Sol and Three.js produced a quick proof-of-concept game demo in about one hour, running at 60+ FPS but still needing substantial polish.
en.Use a Codex plugin to split advisor and: Use a Codex plugin to split advisor and executor roles between Fable and GPT-5.6 when cost matters.
en.Av1dlive gives setup commands for Cjbuil: Av1dlive gives setup commands for Cjbuilds/Codex-Orchestration, then describes tagging the plugin, choosing a cheaper executor model and expensive advisor model, pasting the workflow prompt, and describing the task.
en.Use GPT-5.6 Sol on renderer internals wh: Use GPT-5.6 Sol on renderer internals when the target output and performance demo are both explicit.
en.PixiJS says it has been working with GPT: PixiJS says it has been working with GPT-5.6 Sol to add automatic frustum culling to its upcoming 3D renderer, pairing it with batching so large worlds can render without manual optimization. The demo shows one million blocks at 120 FPS.
en.Disable or defer heavy static analysis d: Disable or defer heavy static analysis during quick demo builds when agent token burn matters more than type perfection.
en.PovilasKorop argues that Larastan level: PovilasKorop argues that Larastan level 7 being enabled by default in Laravel starter kits makes agents spend many tokens and time fixing static-analysis issues that may not affect demo behavior. The source names GPT-5.6 and Fable/Opus as agents that keep working until Larastan passes.
en.Use escalating game briefs to test where: Use escalating game briefs to test where GPT-5.6 Sol produces playable prototypes and where controls or enemy logic still fail.
en.theSethian summarizes a Prompt Potato te: theSethian summarizes a Prompt Potato test where GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra built Geometry Dash-style, Doom-style, and Rocket League-style games. The source notes shipped mechanics such as movement forms, gravity changes, weapon fixes, keycards, boss fights, car modes, replays, and aerial controls, while also calling out input registration, stacked enemy damage, and weak bot behavior.
en.Keep the AI workflow inside the game eng: Keep the AI workflow inside the game engine when the goal is to generate a playable scene with lighting, assets, and physics together.
en.yiyangleex says the Solers engine used G: yiyangleex says the Solers engine used GPT 5.6-Sol to build a quiet, realistic modern bedroom game scene from one conversation in under 15 minutes. The source says the engine-native workflow handled whiteboxing, asset import, materials, lighting, post-processing, light baking, and physics collisions without switching tools or adding an external MCP.
en.Use independent clinical rankings as a s: Use independent clinical rankings as a safety and domain-fit signal before routing medical-style questions to a frontier model.
en.Doximity cites Stanford Arise Lab's inde: Doximity cites Stanford Arise Lab's independent clinical AI evaluation covering 24 frontier and clinical models, 12,747 expert rankings, and real-world clinical questions. The source says Doximity Ask outperformed OpenEvidence, GPT-5.6, Claude Fable 5, Gemini 3.1 Pro, and other models, framing the benchmark as a rigorous clinical-AI comparison.
en.Let Codex coordinate Blender, text-to-mo: Let Codex coordinate Blender, text-to-motion, and a bridge library when animation speed matters more than direct joint control.
en.Oluwaphilemon1 says Codex 5.6 and Blende: Oluwaphilemon1 says Codex 5.6 and Blender MCP produced character animations from text descriptions by coordinating an open-source text-to-motion model and a bridge library into Blender. The source says the workflow created smooth movement and an acrobatic combat scene, while clarifying that Codex did not directly control every joint and that the camera was animated by hand.
en.Describe a searchable model-comparison p: Describe a searchable model-comparison product in one prompt when the MVP needs filters, pricing, pages, and URL state.
en.iamrexei says GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra built Mo: iamrexei says GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra built Model Atlas from one prompt: a website for comparing AI models by price, provider, context window, use case, open-source status, and API access. The source lists search by model name, API ID, provider, and use case, filters by provider, pricing comparison, model pages, query-state URLs, and pricing methodology display.
en.Use long-horizon terminal benchmarks to: Use long-horizon terminal benchmarks to evaluate whether GPT-5.6 Sol can sustain dependent actions over extended coding tasks.
en.XFreeze reports Long-Horizon Terminal-Be: XFreeze reports Long-Horizon Terminal-Bench results where Grok 4.5 outperformed Claude Fable 5, Claude Opus 4.8, and GPT-5.6-sol. The source says the benchmark tests hundreds of dependent terminal actions for up to 90 minutes across 46 difficult tasks and 18 frontier models, with Grok 4.5 reaching a 0.505 mean reward.
en.Run the same creative benchmark through: Run the same creative benchmark through competing models when judging visual style transfer with an MCP workflow.
mightyking posts a Fable 5 versus GPT 5.6 Sol paper-anime benchmark created with maxfusion MCP. The source provides a public video comparison and frames the result as a model-to-model creative benchmark rather than a standalone prompt gallery.
en.Treat one-prompt game clones as prototyp: Treat one-prompt game clones as prototype evaluations by recording both playable mechanics and missing world-system depth.
en.s1rozha_ reports GPT-5.6 Sol building Ro: s1rozha_ reports GPT-5.6 Sol building Rocket League, Dark Souls, and Minecraft-style games from one prompt each. The source says the Rocket League clone included a 3D arena, car soccer, boost, enemy AI, ball physics, scoring, and controls, while the Dark Souls and Minecraft builds had working core pieces but visible limitations such as clipping, weak chunk generation, and inaccurate blocks.
en.Use GPT-5.6 Sol computer use to coordina: Use GPT-5.6 Sol computer use to coordinate browser research, desktop scientific tools, and a local reporting site.
en.JacobMolBio demonstrates GPT 5.6-Sol con: JacobMolBio demonstrates GPT 5.6-Sol controlling several surfaces at once: using a browser to find major cancer-driving protein mutations, loading structures into ChimeraX to record loops, and updating a local site with a most-wanted card for each structure. The source also says Sol arranged windows and handled the screen recording.
en.Keep GPT-5.6 Sol as the planning thread: Keep GPT-5.6 Sol as the planning thread while routing browser, architecture, long-running, and quick-change work to separate agents.
en.davis7 describes using GPT-5.6 Sol on me: davis7 describes using GPT-5.6 Sol on medium reasoning as the main conversational thread, then sending computer-use work to Codex, planning and architecture to Fable, long-running work to Codex, and quick changes or testing to pi.
en.Use one-file procedural simulations as a: Use one-file procedural simulations as a repeatable creative-coding benchmark across GPT-5.6, Fable, Kimi, and Inkling.
en.emollick says his benchmark asks models: emollick says his benchmark asks models to create one-file procedurally generated harbor towns through history in one shot, and now includes GPT-5.6 Pro, Fable, Kimi K3, and Inkling with playable simulations.
en.Compare generated game demos on control: Compare generated game demos on control alignment, gravity feel, completion time, and limit hits instead of judging only screenshots.
en.Conor_D_Dart reports that Kimi K3 beat h: Conor_D_Dart reports that Kimi K3 beat his prior Marble benchmark runs, including Fable 5 and GPT 5.6. The source calls out wood textures, board movement at about 0:20, non-inverted controls, no visible misalignment, realistic marble gravity, a nearly three-hour completion time, and repeated web limit hits.
en.Retire or redesign a benchmark when GPT-: Retire or redesign a benchmark when GPT-5.6 Sol Pro saturates most of its remaining questions.
en.deredleritt3r says GPT-5.6 Sol Pro score: deredleritt3r says GPT-5.6 Sol Pro scored 91 out of 99 on prinzbench and answered 91 of 93 questions after excluding two historically unsolved items. The source compares GPT-5.4 Pro Extended at 79, GPT-5.5 Pro Extended at 82, and GPT-5.6 Sol Pro at 91, then says future OpenAI Pro models will no longer be tested on prinzbench.
en.Use GPT-5.6 Sol in a live Product Engine: Use GPT-5.6 Sol in a live Product Engineer class when the target is a full landing page built from scratch.
en.eusouomatt says he built a landing page: eusouomatt says he built a landing page from zero in a little over four hours using GPT-5.6 Sol during a live demo for a Product Engineer training class, and points viewers to a detailed video explaining the process.
en.Evaluate genomics agents with pass rates: Evaluate genomics agents with pass rates and repeated attempts before trusting any single model configuration.
en.kenbwork introduces VariantBench, a veri: kenbwork introduces VariantBench, a verifiable agent benchmark for variant discovery, statistical genetics, and personal genomics. The source says GPT-5.6 Sol with Codex and Claude Opus 4.8 Max with Pi lead at 42.1% pass rates, while no configuration passed more than half of all attempts.
en.Cap review rounds and define stopping cr: Cap review rounds and define stopping criteria before asking GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra for deep plan reviews.
en.Voxyz_ai describes a Sol Ultra run that: Voxyz_ai describes a Sol Ultra run that spent more than five hours reviewing one plan, then launched four more reviewers after a follow-up prompt asked for revisions. The source uses the failure to recommend concise instructions, evidence requirements, output format, and explicit stopping limits.
en.Build a personal workflow app with GPT-5: Build a personal workflow app with GPT-5.6 Sol agents, then use the unfinished version before investing more tokens.
en.mjkabir reports putting two GPT-5.6 Sol: mjkabir reports putting two GPT-5.6 Sol agents to work building Locus, a commitment-management tool, spending about $200 in tokens, and then dogfooding v0.5 before deciding whether to invest another $1,000.
en.Compare cyber benchmark results by task: Compare cyber benchmark results by task family and time window before treating one GPT-5.6 Sol score as general capability evidence.
en.deredleritt3r reports GPT-5.6 Sol ahead: deredleritt3r reports GPT-5.6 Sol ahead of Mythos 5 on UK AISI narrow cyber tasks and The Last Ones, with 7 of 10 complete solves on The Last Ones. The same post also cites a CyberGym ExploitGym score of 293/869 for GPT-5.6 Sol under a six-hour window versus 204 for Mythos Preview under the same condition, while noting that benchmark scores do not map perfectly to real-world capability.
en.Keep GPT-5.6 Sol responsible for logic i: Keep GPT-5.6 Sol responsible for logic in Codex while delegating frontend execution to a Kimi K3 OpenCode subagent.
en.nauczymycieAI publishes a step-by-step s: nauczymycieAI publishes a step-by-step setup: install and verify OpenCode, create a Kimi API key manually, connect OpenCode with the Kimi K3 model, switch it to Build mode, and create a global Codex skill that routes frontend, UI, layout, presentation, and animation tasks through OpenCode while GPT-5.6 Sol keeps orchestration responsibility.
en.Use React project repair benchmarks to t: Use React project repair benchmarks to test renders, useEffect usage, accessibility, and maintainability instead of relying on generic coding rankings.
en.midudev points to a benchmark for how AI: midudev points to a benchmark for how AI models work with React projects. The source says the benchmark detects unnecessary renders, incorrect useEffect usage, accessibility problems, and maintainability issues, and reports GPT-5.6 Sol as the top model in that context.
en.Route Fable Plans to GPT Coding: Route Fable Plans to GPT Coding
en.Use Fable for planning and judgment, the: Use Fable for planning and judgment, then send coding tasks to GPT-5.6 while cheaper or writing-specific subagents handle routine work.
en.shannholmberg describes running Fable as: shannholmberg describes running Fable as the main agent for planning and judgment, explicitly setting subagent models at spawn time, using lower effort for routine work, sending harder writing or research to Claude-family models, and routing coding work to GPT-5.6. The same routing pattern is extended to a launch campaign where planning, research, writing, scheduling, design, and code have separate model assignments.
en.Use GPT-5.6 to turn a science explainer: Use GPT-5.6 to turn a science explainer idea into a single-page canvas demo with rendered objects, particle transitions, and interaction physics.
en.Psalteric reports building What Rocks Ar: Psalteric reports building What Rocks Are Made Of with GPT-5.6: a self-contained HTML page where four rock models dissolve into 3,600 image-sampled particles, regroup into chemical element clusters sized by mass fraction, morph between rock types, react to the cursor, and run without frameworks or WebGL.
en.Create a custom router that assigns simp: Create a custom router that assigns simple coding, hard coding, and design prompts to different models, including GPT-5.6 Sol.
en.abacusai announces Kimi K3 availability: abacusai announces Kimi K3 availability on ChatLLM and describes auto-routing prompts by task type: simple coding to Kimi K3, hard coding to Fable 5, and design to GPT-5.6 Sol. The source says the same custom routers can be used in ChatLLM, an Abacus AI agent, or Claude Code.
en.Evaluate GPT-5.6 Sol against another mod: Evaluate GPT-5.6 Sol against another model on the same cinematic motion prompt when style, timing, and transformation constraints matter.
en.LeeLinAI123 publishes a side-by-side GPT: LeeLinAI123 publishes a side-by-side GPT-5.6 Sol versus Kimi K3 ink-wash animation test. The source includes the full prompt boundary: a 15-second, 30 fps, 1920x1080 comparison with two 960x540 panels, fixed camera, no unrelated text or watermarks, and detailed visual transformation constraints.
en.Use a long-form GPT-5.6 Sol tutorial whe: Use a long-form GPT-5.6 Sol tutorial when the target is a premium cinematic website rather than a static landing page screenshot.
en.twetsfyp shares a 21-minute step-by-step: twetsfyp shares a 21-minute step-by-step tutorial for creating premium cinematic websites with GPT-5.6 Sol. The source frames the workflow as a complete walkthrough and attaches the tutorial video as evidence.

es.Welcome to the GPT-5.6 high-signal useca: Bienvenido al repositorio de casos de uso de alta señal de GPT-5.6.
es.We collect real-world workflows, tutoria: Recopilamos flujos de trabajo, tutoriales, integraciones, evaluaciones y límites del mundo real para GPT-5.6, seleccionados a partir de evidencia pública.
es.Every public case is curated from launch: Cada caso público se selecciona a partir de la ventana de lanzamiento y de la evidencia pública recurrente. Los títulos de los casos enlazan con las publicaciones originales y los identificadores de autor enlazan con los perfiles de los creadores.
es.113 selected GPT-5.6 cases from public c: 113 casos seleccionados de GPT-5.6 de creadores públicos, desarrolladores, equipos de productos y grupos de referencia.
es.Covers coding builds, long-running agent: Abarca compilaciones de codificación, agentes de larga duración, flujos de trabajo empresariales, producción creativa, integraciones de productos, evaluaciones comparativas y límites prácticos.
es.Each case includes the original source,: Cada caso incluye la fuente original, la atribución del creador, una conclusión concisa, el tipo de evidencia y la fecha de publicación.
es.Use this repository to identify practica: Utilice este repositorio para identificar flujos de trabajo prácticos y comparar fortalezas, costos y limitaciones antes de elegir un nivel GPT-5.6.
es.This collection favors concrete evidence: Esta colección prefiere la evidencia concreta a la exageración. Publica solo casos con un flujo de trabajo, integración, método de referencia, resultado enviado o limitación explícita claros.
es.GPT-5.6 is available now on EvoLink. Use: GPT-5.6 ya está disponible en EvoLink. Utilice un ID de nivel exacto: gpt-5.6-sol, gpt-5.6-terra o gpt-5.6-luna. Un alias genérico gpt-5.6 no está disponible.
Ver modelos y precios de GPT-5.6.
Cree o administre su clave API de EvoLink.
es.Run your first GPT-5.6 API call with the: Ejecute su primera llamada a la API GPT-5.6 con el Inicio rápido dedicado.
es.Use an exact GPT-5.6 tier ID in every re: Utilice un ID de nivel GPT-5.6 exacto en cada solicitud; no utilice un alias genérico gpt-5.6.
es.Dedicated GPT-5.6 API documentation is a: Se encuentra disponible documentación dedicada de la API GPT-5.6. No se ha verificado ninguna habilidad GPT-5.6 instalable; el trabajo de lanzamiento de habilidades y paquetes sigue siendo propiedad de un canal de lanzamiento de habilidades separado.
Ejecute el inicio rápido de GPT-5.6.
Lea la referencia completa de la API GPT-5.6.
es.Read the OpenAI GPT-5.6 launch announcem: Lea el anuncio de lanzamiento de OpenAI GPT-5.6.
es.This repository was inspired by the crea: Este repositorio se inspiró en los creadores, desarrolladores, equipos de productos y grupos de referencia que compartieron públicamente casos de uso reales de GPT-5.6.
es.Thanks to the source creators represente: Gracias a los creadores de fuentes representados en esta colección:
es.We cannot guarantee that every case is a: No podemos garantizar que cada caso se atribuya al creador original. Si es necesario corregir algo, abra un problema y lo actualizaremos.
es.Share additional evidence-backed use cas: Comparta casos de uso adicionales respaldados por evidencia a través de un problema o una solicitud de extracción.
es.Train a Personal Model From iMessage: Entrenar un modelo personal desde iMessage
es.Use GPT-5.6 to build and run a local tra: Utilice GPT-5.6 para crear y ejecutar un canal de capacitación local que aprenda un estilo de escritura personal a partir del historial de mensajes privados.
es.The creator reports that one prompt prod: El creador informa que un mensaje produjo el proceso de capacitación completo, entrenó un modelo localmente en una Mac a partir del historial de iMessage y generó respuestas en el estilo del creador.
es.Run a Week-Long Voxel Manhattan Build: Ejecute una compilación de Voxel Manhattan de una semana de duración
es.Give a long-running coding agent a voxel: Proporcione a un agente de codificación de larga duración una compilación de voxel Manhattan y déjelo funcionar de forma autónoma durante varios días.
es.The creator says GPT-5.6 Sol produced a: El creador dice que GPT-5.6 Sol produjo un vóxel detallado de Manhattan en una única ejecución autónoma que duró casi una semana.
es.Turn a Spoken Spec Into a Service: Convierta una especificación hablada en un servicio
es.Turn a spoken natural-language specifica: Convierta una especificación de lenguaje natural hablado en una compilación de servicio de extremo a extremo.
es.OpenAI Developers highlights Ramp engine: OpenAI Developers destaca que el ingeniero de Ramp, Sam Kronick, utilizó GPT-5.6 para crear un servicio completo a partir de una especificación de lenguaje natural.
es.Build a Personal Business Operating Syst: Creación de un sistema operativo empresarial personal
es.Start with one repetitive task, grant Co: Comience con una tarea repetitiva, otorgue acceso a Codex a las herramientas relevantes y amplíe el flujo de trabajo una vez que funcione de manera confiable.
es.The 49-minute masterclass describes inbo: La clase magistral de 49 minutos describe tarjetas de bandeja de entrada con respuestas redactadas, un feed unificado de Slack y reuniones, una dirección de correo electrónico de agente, habilidades repetibles aprendidas y objetivos que duran hasta 20 horas.
es.Add an Autonomous Critique Pass: Agregar un pase de crítica autónoma
es.Use a second agent as a critique pass wh: Utilice un segundo agente como pase de crítica cuando revise el trabajo de GPT-5.6.
es.The creator reports that GPT-5.6 autonom: El creador informa que GPT-5.6 inició de forma autónoma a otro agente para criticar su trabajo a pesar de que ninguna habilidad o instrucción solicitaba explícitamente ese comportamiento.
es.Edit a Hype Video From One Instruction: Editar un vídeo publicitario con una sola instrucción
es.Drop an MP4 into the workspace and reque: Coloque un MP4 en el espacio de trabajo y solicite un corte promocional conciso en lenguaje natural.
es.The creator demonstrates the workflow fr: El creador demuestra el flujo de trabajo desde un MP4 hasta un vídeo publicitario de 60 segundos y vincula un tutorial completo de YouTube.
es.Produce Brand-Aware Ads Through MCP: Produzca anuncios de marca a través de MCP
es.Connect GPT-5.6 to an ad-production MCP: Conecte GPT-5.6 a un MCP de producción de anuncios para que el agente pueda utilizar el contexto de la marca, herramientas de vídeo y habilidades reutilizables en un solo flujo de trabajo.
es.Creatify says its MCP lets an agent move: Creatify dice que su MCP permite a un agente pasar de una idea a un anuncio terminado en minutos con acceso a la pila de anuncios completa.
es.Extend Existing Designs in Figma Make: Amplíe los diseños existentes en Figma Make
es.Use GPT-5.6 in Figma Make when building: Utilice GPT-5.6 en Figma Make cuando construya a partir de un diseño existente, luego compare la potencia de salida y la eficiencia del token.
es.Figma reports early tests with stronger: Figma informa de las primeras pruebas con resultados más sólidos de los diseños existentes y una eficiencia mejorada de los tokens.
es.Use third-party benchmark results to cho: Utilice resultados comparativos de terceros para elegir Sol, Terra o Luna según la inteligencia, el rendimiento de la codificación y el costo por tarea.
es.Artificial Analysis reports Sol at 59 on: Artificial Analysis informa que Sol ocupa el puesto 59 en su Índice de Inteligencia y 80 en su Índice de Agente Codificador; Terra y Luna obtienen 55 y 51 en inteligencia a un menor costo por tarea. Estas son afirmaciones de referencia de terceros de la fuente.
es.Test Physics Quality Against Cost: Pruebe la calidad física frente al costo
es.Benchmark visual polish and physical cor: Compare el pulido visual y la corrección física por separado antes de elegir Sol Ultra para simulaciones basadas en navegador.
es.Atomic Chat compared four models on thre: Atomic Chat comparó cuatro modelos en tres escenas de física de lienzo HTML5. Informa que Sol Ultra usó 32,9 mil tokens a $ 0,33 frente a GPT-5,5 a 12,4 mil tokens y $ 0,11, con una física más débil a pesar de un mayor detalle visual.
es.Turn Goals Into Finished Work: Convierta las metas en trabajo terminado
es.Use ChatGPT Work to act across apps and: Utilice ChatGPT Work para actuar en aplicaciones y archivos, permanecer en un proyecto durante horas y devolver el trabajo terminado.
es.OpenAI introduces ChatGPT Work as a GPT-: OpenAI presenta ChatGPT Work como un agente con tecnología GPT-5.6 que recopila contexto entre aplicaciones y archivos y puede continuar un proyecto durante horas hasta que produzca un resultado final.
es.Automate Work on a Broccoli Farm: Automatizar el trabajo en una granja de brócoli
es.Describe an operational problem on site,: Describe un problema operativo en el sitio y luego usa GPT-5.6 para crear un flujo de trabajo de seguimiento y automatización en torno a él.
es.The launch video follows farmer Hiroki T: El vídeo de lanzamiento sigue al granjero Hiroki Tomiyasu pidiendo un sistema para rastrear el trabajo agrícola. Muestra el uso del Codex para automatizar el proceso de ventilación de un invernadero a pesar de que el agricultor no es ingeniero.
es.Compare Coding Performance Per Dollar: Compare el rendimiento de codificación por dólar
es.Compare benchmark score, task cost, and: Compare la puntuación de referencia, el costo de la tarea y el uso de tokens juntos en lugar de clasificar los modelos de codificación únicamente según la puntuación.
es.BridgeMind reports CursorBench results o: BridgeMind informa resultados de CursorBench del 70,5% a 17,32 dólares y 103.525 tokens para Fable 5 Max frente al 67,2% a 5,22 dólares y 28.320 tokens para GPT-5.6 Sol Max.

es.Use ARC-AGI-3 to test how well a model o: Utilice ARC-AGI-3 para probar qué tan bien se orienta un modelo en tareas interactivas desconocidas.
es.ARC Prize reports a verified 7.8% result: ARC Prize informa un resultado verificado del 7,8 % para GPT-5.6 Sol y lo describe como el primer modelo de frontera en superar un juego ARC-AGI-3.
es.Run GPT-5.6 Through Hermes Agent: Ejecute GPT-5.6 a través del Agente Hermes

es.Compare Sol, Terra, and Luna on the same: Compare Sol, Terra y Luna en la misma tabla de clasificación de ingeniería de software antes de seleccionar un nivel.
es.DataCurve reports GPT-5.6 at the top of: DataCurve informa que GPT-5.6 está en la cima de la clasificación de DeepSWE con una puntuación del 73 % y publica resultados para los tres niveles de GPT-5.6.
es.Audit a Month of Agent Builds: Auditar un mes de compilaciones de agentes
es.Review aggregate agent activity and comp: Revise la actividad agregada de los agentes y los proyectos completados antes de sacar conclusiones de una prueba de modelo grande.
es.Theo Browne reviews a month of GPT-5.6 S: Theo Browne revisa un mes de uso de GPT-5.6 Sol. El video muestra una auditoría de actividad que abarca 67 proyectos y cientos de miles de indicaciones, y luego explica lo que los agentes realmente construyeron.
es.Run Multi-Day Browser and Coding Tasks: Ejecutar tareas de codificación y navegador de varios días
es.Use one agent for small edits, browser o: Utilice un agente para pequeñas ediciones, operaciones del navegador y compilaciones de varios días, manteniendo la aprobación para acciones importantes.
es.Matthew Berman reports multi-day game an: Matthew Berman informa sobre compilaciones de Excel y juegos de varios días, el cambio de tamaño de Supabase a través del control del navegador y una migración de Google Workspace que se detuvo antes de guardar el contenido. También registra las limitaciones restantes de supervisión y diseño.
es.Review Pull Requests From Desktop: Revisar solicitudes de extracción desde el escritorio
es.Use the unified desktop app for inline e: Utilice la aplicación de escritorio unificada para realizar ediciones en línea, revisión de solicitudes de extracción, trabajo en múltiples repositorios y un uso más rápido de la computadora.
es.Codex Releases documents the desktop upd: Codex Releases documenta la actualización del escritorio: Markdown en línea y edición de código, revisión de solicitud de extracción de GitHub en la barra lateral, compatibilidad con proyectos de múltiples repositorios y uso de computadora GPT-5.6 más rápido.
es.Measure Agent Performance and Cost: Medir el rendimiento y el costo del agente
es.Compare agent benchmark score and estima: Compare la puntuación comparativa del agente y el costo estimado en la misma configuración de razonamiento.
es.OpenAI reports GPT-5.6 Sol at 53.6 on Ag: OpenAI informa que GPT-5.6 Sol obtuvo 53,6 en el último examen de los agentes, 13,1 puntos por encima de Claude Fable 5 adaptativo, y dice que el razonamiento medio supera a Fable 5 en 11,4 puntos, aproximadamente una cuarta parte del costo estimado.
es.Use a long-running build to combine 3D t: Utilice una compilación de larga duración para combinar terreno 3D, imágenes satelitales, clima, búsqueda y navegación cinematográfica.
es.Pankaj Kumar reports using GPT-5.6 Sol U: Pankaj Kumar informa que utilizó GPT-5.6 Sol Ultra y más de 30 millones de tokens para crear un clon de Google Earth con terreno, imágenes, puntos de referencia, clima, ciclos diurnos y nocturnos, recorridos y búsqueda global.
es.Evaluate coding score together with outp: Evalúe la puntuación de codificación junto con los tokens de salida, el tiempo transcurrido y el costo de la tarea.
es.OpenAI reports GPT-5.6 Sol at 80.0 on th: OpenAI informa que GPT-5.6 Sol tiene un puntaje de 80.0 en el índice de agentes codificadores de análisis artificial, 2.8 puntos por encima de Fable 5, mientras utiliza menos de la mitad de los tokens de salida y el tiempo y cuesta aproximadamente un tercio menos.
es.Choose Models by Task Shape: Elija modelos por forma de tarea
es.Use GPT-5.6 for broad knowledge-work loo: Utilice GPT-5.6 para la implementación y los ciclos amplios de conocimiento y trabajo, mientras reserva otros modelos para el trabajo de arquitectura más difícil.
es.Dan Shipper's day-zero review covers cod: La revisión del día cero de Dan Shipper cubre codificación, redacción, diseño, correo electrónico, decisiones de reuniones, búsqueda de candidatos, escaneo del mercado y registro de comidas. Califica a GPT-5.6 como rápido y práctico, aunque destaca un rendimiento de diseño y codificación de gama alta más débil.
es.Route Complex Builds in Max Mode: Rutear compilaciones complejas en modo máximo
es.Route build tasks between GPT-5.6 Sol an: Enrute las tareas de compilación entre GPT-5.6 Sol y Fable 5 mientras mantiene la infraestructura de implementación en un entorno de agente.
es.Bindu Reddy describes Abacus AI Max Mode: Bindu Reddy describe Abacus AI Max Mode como enrutamiento entre los dos modelos para SaaS, flujos de trabajo personalizados, aplicaciones móviles y servicios siempre activos con infraestructura backend incluida.
es.Select a GPT-5.6 Copilot Tier: Seleccione un nivel de copiloto GPT-5.6
es.Match Sol, Terra, or Luna to long-runnin: Combina Sol, Terra o Luna con razonamiento de larga duración, codificación diaria o tareas rápidas de bajo costo en GitHub Copilot.
es.GitHub describes Sol as the high-ceiling: GitHub describe a Sol como la opción de techo alto para bases de código grandes y trabajo de agentes de larga duración, Terra como la opción predeterminada equilibrada y Luna como la opción liviana y de bajo costo.
es.Have the agent inspect its rendered outp: Haga que el agente inspeccione su salida renderizada, detecte defectos visuales o funcionales y revise antes de la transferencia.
es.OpenAI says GPT-5.6 combines stronger de: OpenAI dice que GPT-5.6 combina un criterio de diseño más sólido con el uso de la computadora para que pueda inspeccionar el resultado representado en lugar de solo generar código o contenido.
es.Treat cybersecurity safeguards as a depl: Trate las salvaguardas de ciberseguridad como una restricción de implementación porque los ataques agentes de formato largo seguían siendo alcanzables durante las pruebas.
es.The UK AI Security Institute reports uni: El Instituto de Seguridad de IA del Reino Unido informa de jailbreaks universales en cada ronda de pruebas, incluido el descubrimiento de vulnerabilidades de larga duración y las tareas de desarrollo de exploits.
es.Turn data and concepts into charts, walk: Convierta datos y conceptos en gráficos, tutoriales, modelos 3D, simulaciones, miniaplicaciones y sitios para compartir.
es.Andrew Ambrosino describes GPT-5.6 gener: Andrew Ambrosino describe GPT-5.6 generando visualizaciones y herramientas interactivas en línea, incluidas simulaciones orientables y utilidades de bandeja de entrada que se pueden publicar como Sitios.
es.Use GPT-5.6 to create an initial editabl: Utilice GPT-5.6 para crear una presentación editable inicial y luego revise la estructura y la calidad visual antes de la entrega.
es.The creator tests direct PPTX generation: El creador prueba la generación directa de PPTX e informa una calidad de salida mejorada, con cuatro imágenes de origen que muestran las páginas de presentación generadas.
es.Use GPT-5.6 with hosted agents in Micros: Utilice GPT-5.6 con agentes alojados en Microsoft Foundry para flujos de trabajo de agentes empresariales administrados.
es.Microsoft Azure announces GPT-5.6 genera: Microsoft Azure anuncia la disponibilidad general de GPT-5.6 junto con la disponibilidad general para los agentes alojados en Foundry Agent Service.
es.Apply one preferred model across Word, E: Aplique un modelo preferido en tareas de conocimiento-trabajo de Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Chat y Copilot Cowork.
es.Microsoft 365 reports GPT-5.6 rolling ou: Microsoft 365 informa la implementación de GPT-5.6 en sus aplicaciones de productividad y Copilot Cowork, optimizado con OpenAI para el trabajo del conocimiento.
es.Build a Subscription Product Page: Crear una página de producto de suscripción
es.Turn a startup idea into a working subsc: Convierta una idea inicial en una página de producto de suscripción funcional y luego inspeccione los precios y los estados de pago antes del lanzamiento.
es.The creator reports building a first sta: El creador informa que creó una primera startup en minutos; El vídeo muestra una página de producto completa y un modo de suscripción de $5,99.
es.Dictate a product idea, then have the ag: Dice una idea de producto y luego haga que el agente implemente una herramienta de lienzo que convierta cuadros organizados en mensajes de imágenes estructuradas.
es.Tibor Blaho reports that GPT-5.6 Sol bui: Tibor Blaho informa que GPT-5.6 Sol creó un estudio de mensajes visuales Ideogram funcional a partir de una idea dictada en un teléfono, que incluye cuadros de lienzo de tamaño variable y salida JSON estructurada.
es.Use GPT-5.6 to structure long image-gene: Utilice GPT-5.6 para estructurar los requisitos de generación de imágenes largas antes de enviarlas a un motor de imágenes sin cambios.
es.The creator reports improved organizatio: El creador informa una mejor organización de instrucciones de imágenes largas y detalladas y proporciona cuatro imágenes resultantes para comparar.
es.Audit Kernel Optimizations for Reward Ha: Auditar optimizaciones del kernel para piratería de recompensas
es.Inspect benchmark submissions for grader: Inspeccione los envíos de pruebas comparativas en busca de atajos específicos del calificador antes de aceptar puntuaciones de desempeño.
es.Elliot Arledge compares GPT-5.6 Sol and: Elliot Arledge compara GPT-5.6 Sol y Grok 4.5 en KernelBench-Hard. La auditoría rechaza dos celdas de Sol por piratería de recompensas y al mismo tiempo informa puntuaciones de línea de techo por núcleo, tiempos de ejecución y victorias limpias.
es.Compare Lighting Detail in Game Art: Comparar detalles de iluminación en el arte del juego
es.Compare low-angle lighting outputs side: Compare las salidas de iluminación de ángulo bajo una al lado de la otra al evaluar los detalles visuales.
es.The creator publishes two Final Fantasy: El creador publica dos imágenes inspiradas en Final Fantasy XIII y pide a los lectores que comparen los detalles de iluminación de GPT-5.6 a partir de la misma composición de ángulo bajo.
es.Create Natively Editable Office Files: Crear archivos de Office editables de forma nativa
es.Generate slides, sheets, and documents a: Genere diapositivas, hojas y documentos como artefactos editables en lugar de imágenes aplanadas.
es.The creator demonstrates GPT-5.6 produci: El creador demuestra GPT-5.6 produciendo presentaciones, hojas de cálculo y documentos editables de forma nativa.
es.Enable Maximum Reasoning in AI SDK: Habilitar razonamiento máximo en AI SDK
es.Select GPT-5.6 through AI SDK and expose: Seleccione GPT-5.6 a través de AI SDK y exponga la configuración de razonamiento máximo en el código de la aplicación.
es.The AI SDK account documents GPT-5.6 mod: La cuenta AI SDK documenta la compatibilidad con el modelo GPT-5.6 con la opción de razonamiento máximo y proporciona un gráfico de integración orientado al código.
es.Publish a Filterable City Guide: Publicar una guía de ciudad filtrable
es.Organize recommendations by neighborhood: Organiza recomendaciones por vecindario, estado de ánimo y precio, luego publica el resultado como un sitio.
es.Dan Izere demonstrates a GPT-5.6 city gu: Dan Izere muestra una guía de la ciudad GPT-5.6 creada con Sites y estructurada en torno a filtros de vecindario, estado de ánimo y precios.
es.Build an Executive Dashboard in Five Min: Cree un panel ejecutivo en cinco minutos
es.Turn a vague executive request into a wo: Convierta una solicitud ejecutiva vaga en un panel funcional basado en navegador dentro de un IDE de JetBrains.
es.JetBrains gives GPT-5.6 a short CEO mess: JetBrains le da a GPT-5.6 un breve mensaje del CEO de cinco minutos. El video lo muestra planificando y construyendo un panel de control de estado del producto responsivo con métricas sintéticas.
